title: Mama Gravas

jokes.text: You can keep.
jokes.setup: Someone compliments Latka's nice clothes
jokes.punchline: Latka responds as if he's giving away the clothes instead of accepting the compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Latka misunderstanding of English social conventions, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You like my shirt? Here, take it!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You like my pants? Here, take them too!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: In my country, when someone says they like something, you give it to them. It's polite!

jokes.text: Right at the end of flight.
jokes.setup: Asked when his mother's plane lands
jokes.punchline: Latka gives a technically correct but unhelpfully obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Latka's literal-minded foreign perspective creates gentle humor

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: When does my mother's plane land? It lands when it comes down.

jokes.text: She coming right here. Louie give me rest of day off so I could show her around the Big Banana.
jokes.setup: Latka explains his plans for his mother's visit
jokes.punchline: Calls NYC 'The Big Banana' instead of 'The Big Apple'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the correction joke nicely, good character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: My mother, she comes to visit me here in The Big Banana.

jokes.text: Latka, I think you mean The Big Apple. / Oh, that, too.
jokes.setup: Someone corrects Latka's 'Big Banana' mistake
jokes.punchline: Latka agrees as if NYC has multiple fruit nicknames
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline with good timing and character logic

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: It's the Big Apple, not the Big Banana.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Oh! So that's why they call it the Big Apple. I thought maybe they call it the Big Banana on Tuesdays.

jokes.text: Right, I promised, now I'm breaking my promise.
jokes.setup: Latka reminds Louie that he promised him the day off
jokes.punchline: Louie matter-of-factly admits to breaking promises without shame
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - his shameless honesty about being dishonest is both shocking and funny

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You promised me the day off.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, so?

jokes.text: I'm not nasty. A lot of guys wouldn't have given you the day off in the first place.
jokes.setup: Latka calls Louie nasty for breaking his promise
jokes.punchline: Louie defends himself with twisted logic about being generous
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Louie's self-justification is believably warped

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You're nasty, Louie. You broke your promise to me.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Nasty? I'm generous! I promised you something, and I broke it. Now you got a broken promise instead of a whole one. Which is worth more?

jokes.text: I'm not doing this because I enjoy it. I do enjoy it, but that's not the reason.
jokes.setup: Louie continues defending his actions
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits he enjoys being cruel while claiming that's not why he does it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - the contradiction reveals Louie's psychology perfectly while being incredibly funny

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I'm not cruel! I discipline people because it's good for them, it builds character!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Sure, I enjoy it, but that's not why I do it. I do it because... because they deserve it!

jokes.text: I'll pretend I didn't hear that.
jokes.setup: Louie's shocking admission about enjoying being cruel
jokes.punchline: Someone's understated reaction to this appalling statement
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that acknowledges the absurdity without overplaying it

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: I enjoy being cruel to people.

dialogue.speaker: Alex Reiger
dialogue.line: Yeah, we know.

jokes.text: Poor little Mama, she want to see America before she die.
jokes.setup: Latka tries to guilt Alex into helping his mother
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions his mother's impending death as emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor disguised as innocent guilt trip - Latka's cultural directness about death creates discomfort

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Alex, you help me, yes? My mother, she is very sick. The doctors, they say maybe two, three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Latka, I'm sorry to hear that, but I really can't—

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Is okay, is okay. You don't help. When my mother dies, you think of me, yes?

jokes.text: Mama, my mama!
jokes.setup: Alex suggests Tony could help instead
jokes.punchline: Latka dramatically throws himself at Tony in desperation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that works through Latka's desperation and cultural expressiveness

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Tony! Tony, please! You help me, yes? You help Latka?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: What's wrong, Latka?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I am begging you, Tony! Please!

jokes.text: I think I got a date for breakfast.
jokes.setup: Alex asks Tony a question about helping
jokes.punchline: Tony completely misunderstands the situation as Alex asking him out
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke that shows Tony's obliviousness to the actual situation

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Tony, would you help me with something?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Alex, I'm flattered, but I'm not really looking for a relationship right now.

jokes.text: Oh, garbage strike, blackout, urban blight.
jokes.setup: Alex asks what tourist attractions she wants to see in NYC
jokes.punchline: She lists NYC's problems as tourist attractions
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about NYC's reputation - subverts tourist expectations perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Well, I'd like to see the Statue of Liberty, the Empire State Building, Times Square...

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: And I hear the muggings in Central Park are very popular this time of year.

jokes.text: I'll find out when the next tour is.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Gravas wants to see urban problems as tourist attractions
jokes.punchline: Alex treats this as a reasonable request
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that plays along with the absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: I want to see the urban problems as tourist attractions.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, that's a very reasonable request. We can arrange that for you.

jokes.text: Oh, also want to see Yankee Dog Stadium.
jokes.setup: Continuing the list of tourist attractions
jokes.punchline: Mangles 'Yankee Stadium' into 'Yankee Dog Stadium'
jokes.explanation: Simple foreign language mistake joke - functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Yankee Dog Stadium

jokes.text: You know, you talk very good English. / You, too.
jokes.setup: Alex compliments Mrs. Gravas on her English
jokes.punchline: She returns the compliment to a native speaker
jokes.explanation: Standard ESL humor - Mrs. Gravas doesn't understand the context of the compliment

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Your English is wonderful, Mrs. Gravas.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Thank you. Your English is wonderful too.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, I'm a native speaker.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: I know. That's what makes it so impressive.

jokes.text: You're welcome. / You're welcome from me, too.
jokes.setup: Confusion over 'thank you' and 'you're welcome'
jokes.punchline: Alex gets caught up in the polite loop and makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Minor language confusion joke that extends the running gag slightly

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: No, thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: No, I'm thanking you.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: And I'm saying you're welcome!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: For what?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I don't know anymore!

jokes.text: No thirsty.
jokes.setup: Alex offers her a drink
jokes.punchline: Her broken English response
jokes.explanation: Simple broken English joke without much cleverness

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Can I get you a drink?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: No, no, I don't drink. My husband, he drink all the time. One day he come home, he fall down the stairs. I say, 'Harold, why you fall down the stairs?' He say, 'I didn't fall down the stairs, I was pushed.'

jokes.text: Oh, my, my, you're welcome. / I marry Latka's father when 15.
jokes.setup: Alex compliments her youthful appearance
jokes.punchline: She responds with inappropriate gratitude phrase then reveals she was a child bride
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about her young marriage age, delivered with innocent confusion about English phrases

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Thank you, dear. I owe it all to my husband. He married me when I was just twelve years old.

jokes.text: He killed two years later. / Oh, I'm sorry. / Oh, you're welcome.
jokes.setup: Discussion of her deceased husband
jokes.punchline: She responds to condolences with 'you're welcome' instead of 'thank you'
jokes.explanation: Callback to the phrase confusion but in a darker context about death

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry to hear about your husband.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

jokes.text: Oh, he was a freedom fighter shot by police? / No, he was a police shot by freedom fighter.
jokes.setup: Alex assumes her husband died heroically fighting oppression
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was actually the oppressor who got shot
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts expectations about the noble dead husband

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: My husband died a hero, fighting against oppression and tyranny.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Actually, he was the oppressor. Someone shot him.

jokes.text: In my country, Alexander mean... one who makes nik-nik during harvest.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what Alex's name means
jokes.punchline: Innocent-sounding foreign definition that's clearly sexual
jokes.explanation: Sexual innuendo disguised as innocent foreign language explanation

jokes.text: Well, that's me all right.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Gravas explains his name means someone who has sex during harvest
jokes.punchline: Alex accepts this sexual characterization of himself
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that plays along with the sexual implication

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, I guess that's better than what my name means in English.

jokes.text: And yours fits, too. / It's not even harvest. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: She says his name fits him after the sexual definition
jokes.punchline: Alex points out it's the wrong season for his supposed sexual behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the harvest/sex joke with good timing

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Your name fits you.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: How's that?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Alex. It means sexually active.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, it's the wrong season for that.

jokes.text: Rockets? / Oh, no, no, no. Eh...
jokes.setup: Mrs. Gravas mishears 'Rockettes' as 'Rockets'
jokes.punchline: Latka's embarrassed reaction suggests she thought of something inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Minor misunderstanding joke with sexual undertones

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: I saw the Rockets at Radio City last night.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: The Rockets? Mrs. Gravas, you mean the Rockettes.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: That's what I said, the Rockets.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: No, the Rock-ETTES. They're dancers.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Well, of course they're dancers. What did you think I meant?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Nothing! Nothing at all.

jokes.text: You can take girlfriend. / What about your mother? / She don't have a girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Latka suggests Alex take his girlfriend to the show, Alex asks about Latka's mother
jokes.punchline: Latka misunderstands and says his mother doesn't have a girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection that works through Latka's literal interpretation of pronouns

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: What about your mother? Wouldn't she like to go to the show?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: My mother? No, no, no. My mother doesn't have a girlfriend.

jokes.text: It's always nice to get the news from home.
jokes.setup: Witnessing the bizarre foreign conversation with dramatic reactions
jokes.punchline: Alex's dry comment on the incomprehensible exchange
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man capper to the absurd foreign conversation

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You know, I've heard a lot of languages in this cab, but I have no idea what that was.

jokes.text: The apple machine philosophy
jokes.setup: Someone complains about getting old apples from a machine
jokes.punchline: John creates an elaborate circular logic about why you can never get new apples
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures the character's spacey philosophy - builds to absurd conclusion

dialogue.speaker: John
dialogue.line: Well, think about it. The machine can only dispense apples that are already in the machine, right? And those apples had to come from somewhere. They came from the supplier. And the supplier gets them from the distributor. And the distributor gets them from the grower. By the time they reach you, they've been sitting in trucks, in warehouses, on shelves... So technically, every apple is old. There are no new apples. The apple is picked, and from that moment on, it's aging. So you can never get a new apple from a machine because the only apples available are the ones that already exist, and anything that already exists is, by definition, not new.

jokes.text: So then how do we get new apples, John? / We can't.
jokes.setup: Someone asks for the solution to John's apple paradox
jokes.punchline: John matter-of-factly states there is no solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of hopeless conclusion - economical and character-perfect

dialogue.speaker: John
dialogue.line: There is no solution.

jokes.text: It was sort of like, you know, uh, two people being at the same place at the same time, uh, together. That's all.
jokes.setup: Asked how his 'date' with Latka's mother went
jokes.punchline: Alex's awkward over-explanation that obviously describes a date
jokes.explanation: Good awkward humor as Alex tries to downplay what was clearly a date

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, it wasn't a date. We just... went out to dinner. And then we went to a movie. And then we took a walk in the park. But it wasn't a date.

jokes.text: If she'd just stop wearing those black dresses all the time... shed a few pounds... get rid of the wart and the mustache.
jokes.setup: Tony claims his mother is good-looking
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly unattractive features she'd need to change
jokes.explanation: Standard escalating insult joke with good build-up of increasingly gross details

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: My mother, she's a good-looking woman. But if she wants to look even better, she's gonna have to do a few things. She's gonna have to get rid of that mustache. She's gonna have to lose about ninety pounds. She's gonna have to get her teeth fixed. She's gonna have to do something about that glass eye. And she's gonna have to stop wearing a bra as a hat.

jokes.text: If she'd just stop wearing those black dresses all the time... shed a few pounds... get rid of the wart and the mustache.
jokes.setup: Bobby talks about his attractive mother
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly unattractive features
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bobby's self-delusion about his mother

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: My mother is so beautiful. She's got this incredible face, perfect skin, amazing hair. But then you look at her neck and it's all wrinkled. And her hands? Forget about it—they look like two old baseball gloves. Her legs are all varicose veins. And don't even get me started on her feet.

jokes.text: What if the mother, well, uh... forced herself on the guy? / Forced herself? Yeah. / I mean, what'd she do, like throw him down and pin him to the floor? / Kind of, yeah.
jokes.setup: Alex tries to explain his situation hypothetically
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly physical details about his encounter
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Alex digs himself deeper into TMI territory

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, hypothetically speaking, if a man were to encounter a woman in an elevator...

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Alex, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Just listen. He'd notice she had auburn hair, very soft auburn hair...

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Where is this going?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: And she smelled like lilacs. And when the elevator stopped between floors, hypothetically, she might have...

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Alex!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: And then hypothetically, there was this dress, and it was on the floor of the elevator, and hypothetically my shirt was also on the floor...

jokes.text: I mean, what'd she do, like throw him down and pin him to the floor?
jokes.setup: Elaine sarcastically questions the 'forced' claim
jokes.punchline: Gives an absurdly physical description
jokes.explanation: Sets up the punchline about what actually happened

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Forced? What do you mean forced?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: He had his hand on the back of my neck, his knee was between my legs, and he was making these grunting sounds like a water buffalo.

jokes.text: Latka's mother?!
jokes.setup: Alex admits he slept with Latka's mother
jokes.punchline: Elaine's shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Basic shock reaction that serves the scene structure

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You slept with Latka's mother?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: It just happened.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Alex, that's his mother!

jokes.text: Just for that? / Huh? No, no, no. She flew in to see Latka.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if she flew in just to sleep with Alex
jokes.punchline: Alex's flustered correction reveals his ego was briefly flattered
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Alex's momentary vanity

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Did you think I flew in just to sleep with you?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: No, no, of course not. I mean, yes... I mean, for a second there I thought you did, but obviously you didn't.

jokes.text: About six-foot-three.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks how old Latka's mother is
jokes.punchline: Alex gives her height instead, revealing his nervousness
jokes.explanation: Good nervous comedy through Alex's mental scrambling under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: How old is Latka's mother?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Five foot two.

jokes.text: After we were alone for a while, she turned into an animal. / A great one.
jokes.setup: Alex describes what happened when they were alone
jokes.punchline: Clarifies that being an 'animal' was a positive thing
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual brag with good timing on the clarification

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I was alone with her, and I just... I became an animal.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I mean, I was great! I was really great!

jokes.text: You don't need to explain... Daddy.
jokes.setup: Alex tries to explain his situation to Latka
jokes.punchline: Latka calls him 'Daddy' assuming they're now family
jokes.explanation: Perfectly unexpected and cringey - shows Latka's innocent assumption about relationships

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Latka, I need to explain something to you.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Yes, Alex?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: We're not actually related. I was just trying to help you out.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Oh! So we are family now, yes? Daddy!

jokes.text: If I were you, I'd think of something else.
jokes.setup: Latka approaches as Alex confesses
jokes.punchline: Elaine's understatement about his predicament
jokes.explanation: Good timing and understatement for the awkward situation

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm in terrible trouble.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Well, it's not so bad.

jokes.text: See you later, Papa.
jokes.setup: Latka continues treating Alex as his father figure
jokes.punchline: Casually calls Alex 'Papa' in front of others
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates Alex's embarrassment publicly

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Papa, can you help me with this?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Latka, not in front of everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: But Papa, you always help me.

jokes.text: We are going to be so happy together. Mama cook, Alex read paper, I mow the lawn, oh... Can I have a puppy?
jokes.setup: Latka describes his fantasy family life
jokes.punchline: Ends with childish request for a puppy
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that shows Latka's childlike excitement about having a father figure

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I have beautiful wife, two wonderful children, nice house in country. Every evening we sit together, very happy family. Children do homework, wife she knit the sweater, I read newspaper. Is perfect life.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: But you know what would make even more perfect? Puppy. Can we get puppy, Mama?

jokes.text: We are going to be so happy together... Can I have a puppy?
jokes.setup: Latka fantasizes about their new family
jokes.punchline: Childishly asks for a puppy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Latka's childlike enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Can we get a puppy? Can we? Can we? Please?

jokes.text: But, Mama, if you play nik-nik with Alex, then you must marry. It's the only way to save the family honor.
jokes.setup: Family discussion about marriage expectations
jokes.punchline: References the earlier 'nik-nik' sexual euphemism in a serious context
jokes.explanation: Good callback that brings the sexual euphemism into a serious cultural context

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: In my country, when a man and woman marry, there are expectations.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: What kind of expectations?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: The man, he must provide. He must be strong. And there must be... nik-nik.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Nik-nik?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Yes, nik-nik. Many times. Or the marriage, it is no good.

jokes.text: Oh, goat droppings.
jokes.setup: Latka insists they must marry to save honor
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Gravas dismisses this with a foreign curse
jokes.explanation: Mild ethnic humor with creative euphemistic cursing

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: We must marry! It is a matter of honor!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Gravas
dialogue.line: Bah! Honor? Pffft! Boonta la vista, you meshuga!

jokes.text: If you don't marry Alex, then you're... you're a kakbort.
jokes.setup: Latka continues pressuring for marriage
jokes.punchline: Uses a made-up foreign insult that sounds terrible
jokes.explanation: Made-up foreign word that sounds appropriately harsh for the context

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You marry me now or I tell everyone you are a shnorglwax!

dialogue.speaker: Simka
dialogue.line: Latka, what is a shnorglwax?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I don't know, but it sound very bad in my country!

jokes.text: My mother's off-limits.
jokes.setup: Louie observes the situation with Alex and Latka
jokes.punchline: Warns Alex about his own mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie line - inserting himself into drama with absurd warning

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Alex, let me give you some advice about mothers. I know about mothers. I got a mother. And let me tell you something — watch out for her.

jokes.text: My mother's off-limits.
jokes.setup: Louie witnesses the family drama about Alex and mothers
jokes.punchline: Louie warns Alex away from his own mother
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Louie makes everything about himself even in others' crises

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Listen kid, take some advice from an old man. Stay away from your mother.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: But Louie, she's my mother.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Exactly. That's what I'm talking about.

jokes.text: You mean, not even indoors?
jokes.setup: Alex says what happened was 'indiscreet'
jokes.punchline: Latka misunderstands as 'not discrete' meaning outdoors
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay based on Latka's literal interpretation - shows his innocence and language confusion

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Not discrete? You mean like outside? In the open air?

jokes.text: Brefinish. I make myself. Make a good anti-freeze, let me tell you. No, eats... eats the pipes.
jokes.setup: Alex asks what Latka is drinking
jokes.punchline: Latka describes his homemade alcohol as industrial solvent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from describing alcohol to describing its destructive properties

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: What are you drinking, Latka?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Oh, is my own recipe. I call it 'Latka's Special.' Very smooth, very delicious.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: What's in it?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Well, is mostly industrial solvent, but I add little bit of fruit juice so is not so obvious.

jokes.text: My mother's 67 years old. / Then not do for sure.
jokes.setup: Alex points out Latka wouldn't seduce his mother
jokes.punchline: Latka agrees purely based on age, not principle
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Latka's practical rather than moral reasoning

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You wouldn't seduce my mother.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: No, no, I wouldn't. She's too old.

jokes.text: Not even when the Dallas cheerleaders are on?
jokes.setup: Alex lists activities they'll never do as friends
jokes.punchline: Brings up the most tempting possible scenario for watching football
jokes.explanation: Standard male bonding reference that fits the era and characters

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: We'll never watch football together.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Not even if it's the Super Bowl, and your team is winning, and there's free beer, and Cheryl Tiegs answers the door in a negligee.

jokes.text: Globnik. 'To pretend or assume some event or occurrence never took place.'
jokes.setup: Latka suggests they 'globnik' to solve their problem
jokes.punchline: The foreign solution is simply to pretend it never happened
jokes.explanation: Clever resolution that makes a foreign word for a universal human behavior

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You globnik.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: What does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You pretend it never happened.

jokes.text: Pretend what never happened? / Beats me. / Me, too.
jokes.setup: They agree to globnik (pretend it never happened)
jokes.punchline: They've already forgotten what they're supposed to pretend didn't happen
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic resolution - they've successfully globniked so completely they don't remember what happened

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: So we agree to globnik?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Yes, we globnik.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: What did we globnik?

jokes.text: Abbee, abbee, abbee, abbee, dabbee, dabbee, dabbee, dabbee, eicky, eicky, eicky, eicky, bicky, bicky, bicky, bicky, eh... sappee, sappee, sappee, sappee.
jokes.setup: Latka dealing cards in poker game
jokes.punchline: Uses nonsense foreign card names
jokes.explanation: Pure Latka character comedy with invented card game terminology

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, we got... the... uh... the flibberjabit of spades, the croont-croont of hearts, and the magnificent sploofenplatt of diamonds!

jokes.text: I just missed my royal pipik.
jokes.setup: Continuing poker game in Latka's language
jokes.punchline: 'Royal flush' becomes 'royal pipik'
jokes.explanation: Continues the poker language gag with 'pipik' (belly button in Yiddish)

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Royal pipik!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Latka, that's a royal flush, not a royal pipik.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: In my country, we call it royal pipik. It means the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: What's a pipik?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Pipik is belly button. Royal pipik is... how you say... royal belly button.

tmdb_overview: Latka's visiting mother finds Alex's company more appealing than her son's.
insight: Mama Gravas episode leans on character quirks over setup-punchline structure throughout.
